messages.content:
=== Iteration 1 ===
THOUGHT: I need to list files.

PLAN:

1. Use the 'ls' tool to list directory contents.
2. Optionally, use the 'glob' tool if we want to filter files based on a pattern.

EXECUTE:
 
  <tool_code>{"name": "ls", "arguments": {"path": "."}}</tool_code>
  If desired, use glob tool instead:
  <tool_code>{"name": "glob", "arguments": {"pattern": "*"}}</tool_code>

[ls] → drwxr-xr-x 2  4096 May 25 23:51 vscode-extension
drwxr-xr-x 2  4096 May 06 22:57 dist
drwxr-xr-x 2  4096 May 25 23:27 .ollamadev

[glob] → ./dist
./vscode-extension

=== Iteration 2 ===
THOUGHT: The files in the current directory are "vscode-extension" and "dist". 

PLAN:

1. Use the 'ls' tool to list directory contents.
2. Optionally, use the 'glob' tool if we want to filter files based on a pattern.

EXECUTE:
 
  <tool_code>{"name": "ls", "arguments": {"path": "."}}</tool_code>
  If desired, use glob tool instead:
  <tool_code>{"name": "glob", "arguments": {"pattern": "*"}}</tool_code> 

Since only one file was returned, no changes needed for the execution of 'list_files'.

[ls] → drwxr-xr-x 2  4096 May 25 23:51 vscode-extension
drwxr-xr-x 2  4096 May 06 22:57 dist
drwxr-xr-x 2  4096 May 25 23:27 .ollamadev

[glob] → ./dist
./vscode-extension

drwxr-xr-x 2  4096 May 06 22:57 dist
drwxr-xr-x 2  4096 May 25 23:27 .ollamadev
